title: Harissa Tempeh
subtitle: with Pistachio Pilaf & Zucchini

steps.text: Add the brown rice, 1¾ (3½) cups water, and a pinch of salt to a small saucepan and bring to a boil. Cover, reduce heat to low, and cook until water is absorbed, 30 to 35 minutes.
Halve the zucchini lengthwise and slice into half moons. Peel and thinly slice the shallot(s). Cut the tempeh into 1 inch cubes. Halve and juice the lemon(s).
Add the harissa paste, agave, just half the lemon juice, 1 tbsp (2 tbsp) olive oil, and a pinch of salt in a small bowl. Stir the harissa sauce.

Heat 2 tbsp (4 tbsp) olive oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add the cubed tempeh and cook until crispy, 8 to 10 minutes. Sprinkle tempeh with salt and add the sliced shallot. Cook until the shallot begins to brown, 2 to 4 minutes.

Add the harissa sauce to the skillet. Cook until the tempeh is caramelized and sticky, 2 to 4 minutes. Move the harissa tempeh to the side of the skillet and add the sliced zucchini. Sprinkle with salt and cook until tender, 2 to 4 minutes.
Add the remaining lemon juice and pistachios to the cooked brown rice and stir. Divide the pistachio pilaf between plates. Top with harissa tempeh and zucchini. Dig in!

tips: * This ingredient will be split between steps.
